title: The Clean Truck and Bus Voucher Incentive Project (HVIP) Reopens with $125 Million
content:
The California Air Resources Board (CARB) is reopening the Clean Truck and Bus Voucher Incentive Project (HVIP) on December 16, 2025. Starting at 9:00 a.m. HVIP will begin accepting voucher request applications on a first-come first-served basis. 
Standard HVIP funding is currently fully subscribed, but CARB will reopen the project using funds from the recent Hino settlement and funding from cancelled vouchers.  Approximately $95 million will be directed to Standard HVIP ($5 million of these funds will be in a set-aside for public fleets and tribal governments), $25 million to the Public Transit Bus Set-Aside, and $5 million to ISEF. This funding will help support zero-emission market momentum, meet ongoing fleet demand, and support small business participation. In addition, vehicle eligibility will be expanded to include Class 2b pickup trucks for vouchers requested by state or local government agencies and tribal governments. Eligible tribal governments would include all federally recognized tribes in California and non-federally recognized tribes on the California Tribal Consultation List. Eligible state and local agencies include; cities, counties, public utilities, special districts, and similar public entities.
author: Analysts Account
